title: QUIC Server-Role CertificateVerify Continuation Work Item
summary: Extend the server role from the local Certificate continuation into a permanent local CertificateVerify continuation that accepts an explicit raw P-256 signing-key seam, validates compatibility with the raw-DER leaf certificate seam, appends the local CertificateVerify bytes after local Certificate, exposes the continuation through the existing library seam, and leaves server Finished and wider handshake work unavailable.
planned_changes:
- Extend the server-role handshake continuation so it accepts one narrow raw P-256 signing-key seam only after the supported ClientHello has been accepted, the local ServerHello has been published, local EncryptedExtensions has been published, local Certificate has been published, and Handshake keys are available.
- Reuse the existing bridge-owned transcript seam to append one local CertificateVerify immediately after the local Certificate and expose the bytes through the existing Handshake CryptoDataAvailable path with deterministic offset semantics that place the message after ServerHello || EncryptedExtensions || Certificate.
- Require exact compatibility between the new local signing material and the previously supplied raw-DER leaf certificate seam for the supported subset, keep server-role peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello intact, keep server-role commit unavailable, and reject premature, repeated, conflicting, malformed, or unsupported local CertificateVerify progression deterministically through the existing fatal/update path.
- Add focused requirement-home tests for the positive CertificateVerify continuation, early emission prevention, local signing-material availability gating, deterministic malformed or incompatible material failure cases, repeated/conflicting progression rejection, transcript append and offset semantics, and the continued absence of server-role commit.
- Do not add server Finished, certificate trust-store integration, hostname validation, endpoint-host wiring, harness changes, or interop-runner handshake enablement in this slice.
verification_plan: Run the updated CRT requirement-home tests that cover the positive CertificateVerify continuation path, deterministic malformed or incompatible local signing-material handling, outbound local CertificateVerify availability, offset semantics, local signing-material availability gating, transcript append behavior, and repeated or conflicting progression rejection, then run the relevant solution build and targeted test pass for the main library and tests project.
completion_notes: The server-role CertificateVerify continuation proof is landed. The library now continues from the managed Certificate floor into one local CertificateVerify flight on the existing handshake seam, and server Finished, commit, and wider handshake flight ownership remain explicitly out of scope.
out_of_scope: server Finished, client-certificate authentication, trust-store policy, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, endpoint-host wiring, harness changes, interop-runner handshake support, or any native TLS / QUIC fallback.
